title: Concurrency, Contention, and Distributed Recovery
objective: Prove correct behavior under concurrent approvals, competing writes, duplicate delivery, out-of-order evidence, adapter interruption, and partition-shaped failure.
exit_criteria: Concurrent approval reservation and consumption permit at most one conflicting effect
Duplicate, delayed, and out-of-order messages remain idempotent or fail closed
Compare-and-swap contention and adapter interruption produce reconstructable outcomes
Partition-shaped failures recover without split authority, duplicate effects, or lost receipts
Filesystem and GitHub adapters satisfy the declared concurrency and recovery contract

evidence.description: Implemented exclusive authority leases, concurrent compare-and-swap, duplicate and conflicting delivery handling, partition deferral and healing, interruption recovery, and forensic reconstruction.
Added filesystem and GitHub-content adapter parity, exact retry, conflict denial, interruption recovery, and deterministic reconstruction proof.
Merged the final Sprint 20 adapter parity implementation.
CI passed concurrent contention, duplicate delivery, partition healing, interruption recovery, receipt preservation, and reconstruction tests.
Runtime Coverage passed the core Sprint 20 path matrix.
Work Control Authority passed for the core implementation.
CI passed filesystem and GitHub-content parity, exact retry, interruption recovery, immutable conflict, incomplete reconstruction, and tamper tests.
Runtime Coverage passed every final declared Sprint 20 path.
Work Control Authority passed before final completion promotion.
